blockingIssues.description: The new hydrateRestored() helper still persists session:state via a fire-and-forget async setSecureItem(...) call and RecoveryRestoreScreen navigates away immediately, so an immediate cold kill can still relaunch with stale onboarding/identity flags and misroute the restored wallet back to Welcome or BiometricSetup instead of BiometricUnlock → Main.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated .factory/library/architecture.md so the documented BiometricVault error surface now includes VAULT_ERROR_BIOMETRY_LOCKOUT.

Updated .factory/library/environment.md with the react-native-camera-kit iOS permission-helper quirk: checkDeviceCameraAuthorizationStatus() returns -1 for notDetermined despite its Promise<boolean> typing.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Enforce the skill honesty gate from transcript evidence: do not allow skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true unless required mission/library reads are present in the transcript skeleton and the claimed red-to-green test cycle actually appears before edits.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Six onboarding-ux fix reviews repeated the same discrepancy: sessions 7f7d3c73-ca94-4539-b6f0-9ea9d64d8fbf, cfb4a113-cf16-4fd6-a1b3-0f621143014b, 4d997701-10e1-4c75-80ba-20598f0da38f, e3fc5473-3a11-4250-b875-2da6270d2fb6, 3f6c9c35-d683-4c1d-a6f7-fd935078b2da, and 3eed29ae-fd92-4e3d-8577-e3cc54b4746f all reported followedProcedure=true even though the saved transcript skeletons were missing required .factory/library reads and/or did not show the required red-first test sequence.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the native-module convention so workers know that a small platform-specific legacy bridge (ReactContextBaseJavaModule / isTurboModule=false) is acceptable when a feature explicitly requires an Android-only native shim, even though the default pattern remains TurboModule + codegen.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The mission guidance says native modules should match the existing TurboModule/codegen pattern, but implement-android-flag-secure-native-module legitimately added a non-Turbo EnboxFlagSecure bridge and needed scrutiny to explain why that was acceptable. The concrete behavior is now documented in .factory/library/android-flag-secure-native-module.md, but the normative rule still lives in AGENTS.md.
